summary: Form-associated single-value select with listbox semantics, local search, rich flat options, grouped sections, loading, clearing, and surface-aware trigger chrome.
useWhen: The user must choose one value from a finite set.
The option set benefits from local search, subtext, prefix icons, grouping, or disabled choices.
The selected value must participate in native, Angular, or React form state.
avoidWhen: Several values may be selected at once.
The rows perform commands rather than choosing a form value.
The choices form a hierarchy with expandable parents.

alternatives.when: Several independent values may be selected.
alternatives.reason: SelectMulti owns array values, multi-selection semantics, and count presentation.

alternatives.when: The popup contains commands or state-changing actions.
alternatives.reason: Menu uses action-menu roles and command-focused keyboard behavior.

alternatives.when: A small set of choices should remain visible.
alternatives.reason: RadioGroup exposes all mutually exclusive choices without a popup.

accessibility: Provide aria-label or aria-labelledby when no external label association supplies the accessible name.
The trigger uses combobox semantics and the popup uses listbox and option semantics; do not replace them with menu roles.
Disabled options remain visible, expose aria-disabled, and are skipped by keyboard navigation and typeahead.
Selected options rely on active fill and foreground treatment without a redundant trailing check icon.
Local search moves focus into the search field while preserving the active option through aria-activedescendant.
Use errorMessage with error so the trigger is described by the rendered alert.
states: Value is controlled through the mutable value property; dsChange reports user selection and clearing.
Clearing emits dsChange with an empty string and dsClear, keeps the popup open, and restores useful focus.
Loading replaces the trigger prefix icon, marks the control busy, and temporarily disables option interaction.
The popup search row stays md at every trigger size, using body-medium text, a 20px icon, and option-aligned horizontal insets.
Use options for a flat list or sections for grouped choices; when sections are present they take precedence.
The background prop describes the actual parent surface and uses the same surface set as ButtonUnfilled.
responsiveBehavior: Use width fill in forms and constrained layouts; use hug only when the label-sized control remains discoverable.
The popup matches the trigger minimum width, may grow for content, and clamps to the viewport.
Long option collections scroll inside the popup; virtualization and remote search are not part of this component.

customElements: Assign options and sections through JavaScript properties because arrays and objects cannot be represented safely as attributes.
react: Pass options or sections as wrapper props and handle onDsChange, onDsClear, and onDsOpenChange.
angular: Import DsSelect and SelectValueAccessor when integrating with Angular Forms; bind options and sections as properties.
